title: Estimate and Measure Mass (Grams and Kilograms)
essential_question: How do you estimate and measure the mass of an object?

pretest.question: MASS measures:
choices: A) How much an object weighs (how heavy)
C) How much liquid it holds

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Mass = how much matter / how heavy.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Which is best measured in GRAMS?

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Which is best measured in KILOGRAMS?
choices: C) A backpack full of books

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: What Is Mass?
learn.content: MASS is how much MATTER an object has. Heavier objects have more mass. Mass is measured with a BALANCE or SCALE. The basic units are GRAM (g) and KILOGRAM (kg).

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Gram
learn.content: A GRAM (g) is a small unit. Examples: a paperclip is about 1 g. A coin is about 5 g. A small bag of chips is about 30 g. Grams measure LIGHT objects.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Kilogram
learn.content: A KILOGRAM (kg) is a bigger unit. 1 KILOGRAM = 1,000 GRAMS. Examples: a textbook is about 1 kg. A small dog is about 5 kg. A bag of rice is about 2 kg. Kilograms measure HEAVIER objects.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Choose the Right Unit
learn.content: USE GRAMS for: small light items (coin, eraser, candy). USE KILOGRAMS for: heavier items (backpack, watermelon, person, dog).

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Estimate Mass
learn.content: ESTIMATE = a smart guess. To estimate: compare to something you know. A grape is about 5 g. An apple is about 200 g. A textbook is about 1 kg.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Convert Between Grams and Kilograms
learn.content: TO CONVERT KG TO G: multiply by 1000. Example: 4 kg = 4000 g. TO CONVERT G TO KG: divide by 1000. Example: 5000 g = 5 kg.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Use a Balance
learn.content: A BALANCE compares two masses. Place the object on one side, mass-pieces on the other. When the sides BALANCE (level), the masses are equal. The mass-pieces' total = the object's mass.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today: (1) MASS = heaviness. (2) GRAM = small unit. KG = big unit. (3) 1 kg = 1000 g. (4) Use g for light, kg for heavy. (5) Convert: kg×1000=g; g÷1000=kg.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
